data.title: Psychedelic drug developer Lykos could get lifeline from former Tesla board member
data.description:
Lykos Therapeutics, the developer of psychedelic drugs for mental health conditions that has been in financial difficulty, could get rescued by a former Tesla (TSLA) board member seen as close to Elon Musk.

Antonio Gracias, who also supported Musk's acquisition of Twitter, has made an offer to take a controlling stake in Lykos, which is privately held, in exchange for $30M in equity and $70M in secured debt, according to the Financial Times. He is also seeking three board seats, with the other two going to the Multidisciplinary Association for Psychedelic Studies, which founded the company.

Lykos laid off 75% of its workforce in August 2024 after the U.S. FDA declined to approve midomafetamine, an MDMA-based drug to treat PTSD. The FDA asked the company for an independent review of its prior trials and to conduct a new study.

Gracias' strategy is to have Paragon Biosciences help Lykos get midomafetamine approved and then launch it, the FT reported, citing sources.

MAPS President Rick Doblin has backed the plan as well.

However, Lykos' principal shareholder, Helena Special Investments, has also offered a plan that would provide Lykos a bridge loan of $20M-$30M, according to the newspaper.

Lykos' board is slated to meet Friday to discuss both proposals.

Publicly traded psychedelic-focused drug developers include Mind Medicine (NASDAQ:MNMD), Atai Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATAI), COMPASS Pathways (NASDAQ:CMPS), Cybin (NYSE:CYBN), and GH Research (GHRS).

Nano-caps include 180 Life Sciences (ATNF), Enveric Biosciences (ENVB), Seelos Therapeutics (SEEL), SciSparc (SPRC), Bright Minds Biosciences (DRUG), and Clearmind Medicine (CMND).

data.title: Gene editing stocks drop as Intellia announces layoffs
data.description:
Shares of gene editing companies dropped on Friday as Wall Street reacted to Intellia Therapeutics' (NASDAQ:NTLA) plans to lay off 27% of its staff alongside a decision to halt a mid-stage clinical program targeted at a rare genetic disorder.

While Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) reached a new 52-week low, its rivals, LENZ Therapeutics (LENZ), Verve Therapeutics (VERV), Prime Medicine (PRME), Editas Medicine (EDIT), Sangamo Therapeutics (SGMO), and CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) were also among notable decliners.

The Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech's latest round of layoffs coincided with its decision to halt the development of its CRISPR candidate NTLA-3001, which is targeted at alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency-associated lung disease.

It comes roughly a year after the company's previous strategic review, which led to the departure of approximately 15% of its workforce and a pause in certain research-stage programs.

Despite an $8M charge attributed to the reorganization in Q1, Intellia (NTLA) said its cash balance of ~$862M at the end of Q4 2024, as well as cost savings generated from layoffs, would extend its cash runway through H1 2027 when its first U.S. commercial launch is anticipated.

Intellia (NTLA) is not the only gene editing firm to lay off staff in recent weeks. Its CRISPR rival Editas Medicine (EDIT) waved goodbye to approximately 65% of its staff in December as it shelved reni-cel, its lead gene-editing candidate for sickle cell disease, after a commercial partner didn't come forward to develop it.

data.title: Korea has the most oversold equities around the world – BofA
data.description:
South Korean equities (NYSEARCA:EWY) are currently the most oversold around the world, down 17.7% from their 200-day moving average, followed by Brazil (EWZ) and Portugal (NQPT).

Turmoil shaken the country as President Yoon Suk Yeol is facing insurrection charges after declaring martial law. Officials failed to arrest Yeol initially and are debating more forceful efforts to capture him.

On the market side, Korea’s benchmark KOSPI Index (KOSPI) is down 12.99% from the past six months, although up 4.41% year-to-date so far.

data.title: Watch these 10 large-cap banks in 2025, among other predictions from Barclays
data.description:
Among Barclays' predictions for bank stocks in 2025, the firm sees stocks driving higher, EPS growth accelerating, and multiples expanding.The firm's equity research team covering large-cap banks, headed by Jason Goldberg, issued 10 top-10 lists for the sector in 2025. Here are a few of them:

Top 10 large cap bank predictions for the year:

  1. Bank stocks will climb, outperforming the S&P 500;
  2. The median bank's operating earnings will increase at a double-digit rate after modest growth in 2024;
  3. Net interest income and fee income will rise at the median bank for the first time since 2019;
  4. Industry-wide loans and deposits will increase by a greater amount than in 2024;
  5. The investment banking fee pool will rise by more than 20% for the second straight year;
  6. The median bank will generate positive operating leverage after two straight years in negative territory;
  7. Industry-wide net charge-offs will be little changed;
  8. Most banks will boost dividends and the pace of share repurchases for Barclays' bank composite will accelerate;
  9. The heads of the FDIC, OCC, CFPB SEC, FTC, FHFA, and the Fed vice chair for supervision will change early in 2025 (which had already been reported). Wells Fargo's (NYSE:WFC) asset cap will be lifted;
  10. At least three bank acquisitions with a deal value over $2B will be announced in 2025, after no such transaction in 2024.

Top 10 risks for bank stocks:

  1. Economy: 2% GDP growth, 4% unemployment, and steeper yield curve aren't guaranteed;
  2. Election impact uncertainty: While lower regulation is expected, policy details are unclear and timing is uncertain;
  3. Import tariffs and/or immigration restriction: Implementation of the campaign promises could affect the economy, loan growth, asset quality, and inflation/interest rates;
  4. Asset quality: Keep an eye on lower-end consumer, commercial real estate (especially office), leverage lending, and pockets of commercial & industrial.
  5. Loan growth may not rebound after several quarters of subdued growth;
  6. Deposit betas fared worse than expected in the past tightening cycle;
  7. Competitive landscape: The country's 4,517 banks are also competing with non-banks, fintech, big tech, and crypto;
  8. The Russia/Ukraine conflict, the Middle East, and China top the geopolitical risks;
  9. Cyberattack risks have increased, especially amid geopolitical tension and AI advancement; and
  10. Secular shift: The rise of hybrid work, the transition away from fossil fuels, the accelerating use of AI, and evolving non-bank financial institutions could all have implications.

Top 10 large cap bank stocks to watch in 2025:

  1. Citigroup (NYSE:C): Achieving its 11%-12% ROTCE target in 2025 will require higher revenue, strict expense discipline, and increased share repurchases;
  2. Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC): It's the only large-cap bank stock to decline over the past four years; Goldberg sees shareholders anxious for improvement after the sale of its insurance unit, balance sheet restructuring, and cost-cutting measures in 2024;
  3. Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) could see its asset cap lifted after seven years in place;
  4. JP Morgan Chase (NYSE:JPM): Uses for mounting excess capital are in question; CEO Jamie Dimon's special incentive hits next year;
  5. KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY): After Scotiabank (BNS) took a 14.9% stake in the company, swap/Treasury maturities and securities portfolio restructuring should boost net interest income more than 20%;
  6. U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) called for "meaningful positive operating leverage" at its September investor Day;
  7. PNC Financial Services (NYSE:PNC) has been upbeat on its growth prospects, but has also made successful acquisitions;
  8. Ally Financial (NYSE:ALLY) : "After being the worst stock price performer last year as retail auto asset quality and its net interest margin disappointed, investors are trying to gauge 2025 as CEO Michael Rhodes tackles his first full year," Goldberg wrote.
  9. Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS): A revamped management team is set to tackle persistent expense pressures as it combats recent relative underperformance;
  10. Citizens Financial Group (NYSE:CFG) has "several balls in the air" — balance sheet optimization, wealth management build-out, NYC metro expansion, top 10 initiative, swap program, and relatively large office CRE book.

data.title: Gilead upgraded by Morgan Stanley on lenacapavir potential
data.description:
Morgan Stanley has upgraded Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) to overweight from equal weight, citing the company's HIV drug lenacapavir.

The investment bank said it was "leaning into the bull case," seeing "the potential for upward estimate revisions on lenacapavir for PrEP and further multiple expansion as the company makes progress with its next-gen HIV treatment strategy."

Morgan Stanley added that the most significant risk to its call would be potential policy changes to Medicaid budgets that impact HIV drugs.

The bank raised its price target for the stock to $113 from $87.

data.title: Delta soars to 52-week high despite operational issues in snowy Atlanta
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) is having a strong day on Wall Street, with shares up more than 9%. However, the airline company has had some operational headaches in Atlanta, where a winter storm has forced the closure of five runways.

The carrier also had to suspend the takeoff of flight 2668 from Atlanta to Minneapolis-St. Paul after an indication of an engine issue. Four passengers were reported to have had minor injuries.

Investors looked past the issues in Atlanta after Delta Air Lines' (NYSE:DAL) 2025 guidance topped expectations amid strong demand trends over the last six weeks. CEO Ed Bastian said he expects the full year could be the company's best in its history. "We feel quite good coming into the new year," Bastian said in a media interview. "Everywhere, we see consumers continue to prioritize experience over goods," he added.

Shares of Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) traded at a new 52-week high of $68.98 earlier in the session. The airline stock was up 9.45% at 1:00 p.m. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on Delta (DAL) has been flashing Buy since October.

data.title: Apple's foldable iPhone might face shipping challenges: TF
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) foldable iPhone, which is currently in the planning stage, is only expected to support eSIM due to its ultra-thin design, which could result in shipping challenges, according to TF Internation Securities analysts Ming-Chi Kuo.

"Given that the Chinese market currently does not promote phones that only support eSIM, these two models could face shipping momentum challenges unless their design is modified," Kuo said in a post today on Medium.

"An eSIM is an industry-standard digital SIM that allows you to activate a cellular plan from your carrier without having to use a physical SIM," according to Apple. "You can install eight or more eSIMs on an iPhone and use two phone numbers at the same time."

Apple points out that the eSIM on an iPhone is not offered in mainland China. Instead, Dual SIM is required, which is two nano-SIM cards.

"Although the ultra-thin iPhone 17 may ship in higher volumes than the iPhone Plus, it likely won’t significantly boost iPhone sales overall, partly due to downgraded components paired with a high price and a user experience similar to current models," Kuo said.

Overall, Kuo forecasts that total iPhone shipments might fall 8% to 10% less than the consensus.

"Even with the new iPhone SE4 (expected to launch around mid-1H25), iPhone 1H25 shipments are projected to decline by about 6% YoY," Kuo added.

Barclays analysts echoed this sentiment in a research note on Thursday, saying that 2025 will start out "mixed at best" when Apple reports its all-important fiscal first-quarter results on Jan. 30. After the earnings release, the analysts said they believe there will be "some" iPhone recovery, as the company's installed base gets older, but numbers will be "choppy" throughout 2025.

Apple shares slid about 3% during afternoon trading on Friday.

data.title: Citi anticipates further global growth in 2025
data.description:
The Citi Research team indicated on Friday morning that they expect a strong and positive year for global growth, citing favorable economic conditions and ongoing market trends.

"Global equities rose in 2024, but gains were concentrated in the US and its tech giants. In 2025, we expect equities to rise once again, forecasting 10% upside for the MSCI AC World," Citi Research said in an investor note.

While the financial firm is optimistic about global growth, it highlighted that it continues to maintain an overweight position in both the U.S. and continental Europe.

Citi went on to highliht that it remains balanced in its global portfolio stance. From a sector-by-sector vantage point, it spotlighted that it upgraded Health Care to overweight, joining Communication Services and Financials. At the same time it downgraded Consumer Discrtionary, Utilities, and Industrials to underweight.

As for what lies ahead for global growth, Citi Research outlined three key themes: "Navigating Trump 2.0, broadening EPS [along with] broadening markets,[and] rates debate."

For investors looking to keep a pulse on the global market, outlined below are a handful of exchange traded funds that provide global exposure.

Global ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VTI), (NYSEARCA:SPGM), (QWLD), (URTH), (VT), (NYSEARCA:IOO), (DFAI), (IDV), (VGK), (EZU), (BBEU), (FEZ), (IEV),

data.title: Bayer MRI contrast agent gadoquatrane meets goals in phase 3 trials
data.description:
• Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) said that phase 3 data on its experimental MRI contrast agent gadoquatrane met primary and secondary endpoints in phase 3 trials.
  • The QUANTI studies examined gadoquatrane at a gadolinium dose of 0.04 mmol Gd/kg body weight, a gadolinium dose reduction of 60% compared to macrocyclic gadolinium-based contrast agents dosed at 0.1 mmol Gd/kg body weight.
  • Diagnostic efficacy endpoints were met with the lower gadoquatrane dose. Gadoquatrane met the primary and main secondary efficacy endpoints assessing visualization parameters and lesion detection.

data.title: Oil is the most oversold asset globally – BofA
data.description:

Oil also had the highest ranked returns in 2024, up 2.2%, and hedge fund positioning for oil futures (CL1:COM) has turned bullish, according to BofA Bull & Bear Indicator.

On the other hand, U.S. equities (SP500) is the most overbought asset.
